title: ziziphora granatensis subsp. alpina
intro: Ziziphora granatensis subsp. alpina is a subspecies of the Ziziphora granatensis plant, commonly known as purple mint or Spanish lavender, which is a type of herbaceous perennial plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and carminative effects. The health benefits of Ziziphora granatensis subsp. alpina include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and alleviate stress and anxiety. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to treat various health issues or added to skincare products for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
